documents.title: GRC Professional Workday Analysis — July 20 OSINT Sweep
documents.executiveSummary: Ironintel and Ironwatch live OSINT for July 20, 2026 pivots from BOD 26-04 deadline-day orchestration to post-deadline forensic verification and a new perimeter-appliance zero-day campaign: SonicWall SMA 1000 Series CVE-2026-15409 (CVSS 10.0 SSRF) and CVE-2026-15410 (CVSS 7.2 command injection) are actively chained by Inc ransomware and UTA0533 (Volexity) for unauthenticated root access, credential/TOTP seed harvesting, and VPN-less Active Directory lateral movement to domain controllers — CISA KEV July 14 with BOD 26-04 deadline passed July 17. SharePoint CVE-2026-58644 (CVSS 9.8 deserialization RCE) FCEB deadline passed July 19 — Ironwatch requires IIS machine-key rotation only after artifact eviction; patching alone insufficient per CISA forensics triage guidance. CMMC Phase II C3PAO suspension enters day seven of 60-day reform review with CMMC Reform Task Force report due September 11, 2026; Phase I NIST SP 800-171 Rev 2 self-assessments remain binding under DFARS 252.204-7012. JadePuffer agentic ransomware (Sysdig TRT) and Kudankulam/fairlife supply-chain carryover persist. FAR Council CUI proposed rule public comment closes July 23 — three days remaining.
keyFindings: SonicWall SMA zero-day campaign (CVE-2026-15409 CVSS 10.0 SSRF + CVE-2026-15410 command injection): Rapid7 MDR and Volexity UTA0533 confirm active chained exploitation since June 22, 2026 — Inc ransomware group harvesting credentials, session databases, and TOTP MFA seeds; VPN-less AD authentication from appliance internal IP to domain controllers; CISA KEV July 14 with BOD 26-04 deadline passed July 17 — Technology, Defense, Manufacturing, Healthcare, and Finance Industry Profiles must complete forensic eviction (not patch-only) on internet-facing SMA 1000 appliances.

Post-deadline BOD 26-04 verification day one (July 20, 2026): SharePoint CVE-2026-58644 (CVSS 9.8 deserialization RCE) FCEB deadline passed July 19 — CISA confirms post-exploitation includes IIS machine-key theft, deserialization persistence, and malware deployment across chained CVE-2026-56164/CVE-2026-32201/CVE-2026-45659 campaign; Ironwatch requires artifact hunt and web-shell eviction before IIS machine-key rotation — Finance, Healthcare, Technology, and Public Sector profiles must not close remediation tickets without forensic triage receipts.

Post-deadline BOD 26-04 verification (July 20, 2026): FortiSandbox CVE-2026-39808 and CVE-2026-25089 (CVSS 9.1–9.8) and Oracle EBS CVE-2026-46817 (CVSS 9.8) FCEB deadlines passed July 18–19 — Ironwatch requires attestation of patch deployment, payment-processor API key rotation, and internet-exposed inventory clearance before closing remediation tickets.

Volexity UTA0533 tradecraft disclosure (July 17, 2026): Threat actor combined SonicWall SMA zero-days with tcpdump LDAP credential sniffing and appliance-specific malware — earliest compromise observed June 22; Rapid7 warns patching alone may be insufficient if actors maintain persistence and revert hotfixes — Defense and Technology Industry Profiles must treat perimeter VPN appliances as tier-zero identity infrastructure.

CMMC Phase II suspension day seven (DoW July 13, 2026): Mandatory Level 2 C3PAO certification at award remains suspended with 60-day CMMC Reform Task Force review active; report due September 11, 2026; Phase I self-assessments, SPRS affirmations, and NIST SP 800-171 Rev 2 controls remain in force under False Claims Act Civil Cyber-Fraud Initiative scrutiny — Defense Industry Profile must not defer control implementation during reform window.

JadePuffer agentic ransomware carryover (Sysdig TRT, July 2026): LLM-autonomous end-to-end extortion via Langflow CVE-2025-3248 — Technology Industry Profile must inventory internet-exposed Langflow/LLM orchestration endpoints and restrict AI-agent egress.

KNX building-automation KEV carryover: CVE-2023-4346 (CVSS 7.5 device lockout on KNX Connection Authorization Option 1) FCEB deadline July 29, 2026 — nine days remaining — Manufacturing, Healthcare facility-management, and Public Sector building-control profiles must inventory KNX deployments on BAS/ICS-adjacent networks.

Kudankulam critical-infrastructure contractor breach carryover (disclosed July 17, 2026): World Leaks ransomware syndicate published 1.2TB Reliance Infrastructure data including 14.3GB Kudankulam Nuclear Power Plant contractor records via Yotta Data Services cloud — third-party supply-chain attestations remain open for Manufacturing, Defense-adjacent, and Public Sector profiles.

Fairlife OT ransomware carryover (SEC Form 8-K July 16, 2026): Coca-Cola dairy subsidiary production halt across US demonstrates manufacturing OT incident velocity; correlate with Kudankulam contractor breach for supply-chain resilience attestations.

FAR Council CUI proposed rule (June 23, 2026): NIST SP 800-171 Rev 3 and 72-hour CUI incident reporting via clause 52.240-7 — public comment closes July 23, 2026 (three days remaining); dual-track Rev 2 CMMC self-assessment and prospective Rev 3 FAR alignment required for Public Sector and Defense profiles.

NIST CSF 2.0 SP 1308 final (March 23, 2026): Cybersecurity, Enterprise Risk Management, and Workforce Management Quick-Start Guide aligns FY2026 IG FISMA reporting metrics to CSF 2.0 six functions — Public Sector profiles should map post-deadline BOD 26-04 verification to Govern/Respond subcategories without conflating voluntary CSF with mandatory SP 800-53 controls.

Ironintel correlation priority July 20: SonicWall SMA forensic eviction, SharePoint IIS machine-key rotation sequencing, FortiSandbox/Oracle EBS post-deadline verification, CMMC Phase II suspension governance memo, JadePuffer Langflow inventory — all external research bytes require Irongate DMZ validation before tenant CRM persistence.

industryProfiles.narrativeSummary: July 20, 2026 Finance GRC faces SonicWall SMA zero-day forensic eviction on perimeter VPN estates and SharePoint CVE-2026-58644 post-deadline IIS machine-key rotation sequencing on collaboration planes holding treasury attestations — board packets must cite BIGINT-cent ALE without float drift and Irongate-sanitized incident ingress.

July 20, 2026 healthcare OSINT: SharePoint CVE-2026-58644 post-deadline forensic verification on on-premises PHI repositories; SonicWall SMA appliance compromise on remote-access estates; KNX building-automation lockout on clinical facility BAS networks — OCR and CIRCIA enforcement triggers remain active.

SonicWall SMA zero-day KEV post-deadline orchestration; Volexity UTA0533 appliance-specific malware tradecraft; JadePuffer agentic ransomware via Langflow CVE-2025-3248 demands internet-exposed LLM orchestration inventory and egress controls; Kudankulam Yotta cloud third-party breach validates SaaS supply-chain due diligence frameworks.

CMMC Phase II C3PAO suspension enters day seven of 60-day reform review — Defense contractors maintain Phase I self-assessment integrity under False Claims Act scrutiny; SonicWall SMA zero-day elevates DFARS 252.204-7012 incident reporting velocity on CUI-handling remote-access estates; NIST SP 800-171 Rev 2 SSP, SPRS, and POA&M must align with post-deadline BOD 26-04 verification.

FCEB agencies operate under post-deadline BOD 26-04 forensic verification for SharePoint CVE-2026-58644 and SonicWall SMA zero-days; FortiSandbox and Oracle EBS post-deadline attestation; KNX building-automation due July 29; FAR Council CUI proposed rule comments due July 23 — three days remaining.

July 20, 2026 Manufacturing OSINT: fairlife OT ransomware production halt and Kudankulam contractor critical-infrastructure breach demonstrate supply-chain and OT convergence; SonicWall SMA zero-day on plant perimeter VPN appliances; KNX building-automation lockout on plant BAS networks elevates ICS-adjacent network segmentation requirements.

documents.title: SaaS Disruption Memorandum — July 20 SonicWall Zero-Day Post-Deadline Verification
documents.executiveSummary: July 20, 2026 OSINT confirms SonicWall SMA zero-day chained exploitation (CVE-2026-15409/CVE-2026-15410) alongside SharePoint IIS machine-key post-deadline forensic verification — exposing structural gaps in seat-based GRC SaaS that cannot absorb perimeter-appliance root compromise triage, VPN-less AD lateral movement attestation, or machine-key rotation sequencing within 72-hour board-report cycles. Sovereign control planes with Irongate ingress and integer-cent registers win procurement when federated identity exposure, appliance-as-backdoor tradecraft, and defense certification timeline whiplash converge on post-deadline operational reality.
keyFindings: SonicWall SMA unauthenticated SSRF-to-command-injection chain proves perimeter VPN appliances as tier-zero identity infrastructure — seat-based compliance SaaS cannot orchestrate BOD 26-04 forensic eviction on appliances actors use for VPN-less domain controller authentication at post-deadline velocity.

SharePoint CVE-2026-58644 IIS machine-key theft proves deserialization persistence exceeds patch-ticket workflows — seat-based GRC SaaS cannot orchestrate artifact-eviction-before-key-rotation attestation across on-premises collaboration estates at post-deadline FCEB verification speed.

Volexity UTA0533 appliance-specific malware proves threat actors maintain persistence beyond hotfix application — agentic GRC with Irongate-sanitized OSINT ingress replaces static vulnerability spreadsheets for multi-day perimeter-appliance forensic cycles.

CMMC Phase II suspension day seven creates certification timeline whiplash — seat-based compliance SaaS cannot reconfigure POA&M workflows within 60-day reform window without agent-orchestrated SSP and SPRS delta tracking.

Flat annual platform fees in whole-cent BIGINT displace per-seat models when agentic workforce scales OSINT-to-board-report pipelines across SonicWall forensic eviction, SharePoint machine-key rotation, FortiSandbox post-deadline verification, and CMMC reform memos simultaneously.

industryProfiles.narrativeSummary: Incumbent SaaS renewals face scrutiny when Ironframe demonstrates SonicWall/SharePoint post-deadline forensic verification, deterministic board-report ALE, and Irongate-sanitized OSINT without spreadsheet exports.

Fairlife OT ransomware and Kudankulam contractor breach accelerate Manufacturing SaaS disruption; SonicWall perimeter zero-day and KNX BAS lockout demand agentic GRC replacing manual OT-to-audit bridges at post-deadline BOD 26-04 verification speed.

Fastest SaaS disruption velocity; Volexity UTA0533 SonicWall tradecraft, JadePuffer agentic ransomware on Langflow, and Yotta cloud third-party breach patterns demand agentic GRC to replace manual Jira-to-audit bridges while executing post-deadline KEV verification at perimeter-appliance speed.

ragChunks.section: Active Threat Vector — SonicWall SMA Zero-Day Chain (Post-Deadline Verification)
ragChunks.text: Ironwatch OSINT 2026-07-20: CISA KEV CVE-2026-15409 (CVSS 10.0 SSRF) and CVE-2026-15410 (CVSS 7.2 command injection) on SonicWall SMA 1000 Series actively chained by Inc ransomware and Volexity UTA0533 since June 22, 2026. Rapid7 MDR confirms credential/TOTP seed harvesting and VPN-less Active Directory authentication from appliance internal IP to domain controllers. BOD 26-04 deadline passed July 17 — post-deadline forensic eviction required; patching alone insufficient. Technology, Defense, Manufacturing, Healthcare, Finance, and Public Sector profiles must complete appliance forensic triage before closing remediation tickets.

ragChunks.section: BOD 26-04 Post-Deadline — SharePoint IIS Machine-Key Verification
ragChunks.text: Ironwatch OSINT 2026-07-20: SharePoint CVE-2026-58644 (CVSS 9.8 deserialization RCE) FCEB deadline passed July 19, 2026. CISA confirms post-exploitation includes IIS machine-key theft, deserialization persistence, and malware deployment across chained CVE-2026-56164/CVE-2026-32201/CVE-2026-45659 campaign. Ironwatch requires artifact hunt and web-shell eviction before IIS machine-key rotation — patching alone will not evict attackers holding stolen keys. Finance, Healthcare, Technology, and Public Sector Industry Profiles must not close SharePoint KEV items without forensic triage receipts.

ragChunks.section: Active Threat Vector — Volexity UTA0533 SonicWall Tradecraft
ragChunks.text: Ironintel OSINT 2026-07-20: Volexity disclosed UTA0533 threat actor combining SonicWall SMA zero-days with tcpdump LDAP credential sniffing and appliance-specific malware (disclosed July 17, 2026). Earliest compromise June 22, 2026. Rapid7 warns actors may maintain persistence and revert hotfixes after patch application. Defense and Technology Industry Profiles must treat internet-facing VPN appliances as tier-zero identity infrastructure requiring forensic eviction, not patch-only remediation.

ragChunks.section: BOD 26-04 Post-Deadline — FortiSandbox and Oracle EBS Verification
ragChunks.text: Ironwatch OSINT 2026-07-20: FortiSandbox CVE-2026-39808 and CVE-2026-25089 (CVSS 9.1–9.8) and Oracle EBS CVE-2026-46817 (CVSS 9.8) FCEB deadlines passed July 18–19, 2026. BOD 26-04 mandates forensic triage attestation — verify patch deployment, payment-processor API key rotation, and internet-exposed inventory clearance before closing remediation tickets. Finance, Healthcare, and Public Sector Industry Profiles must not mark KEV items closed without forensic receipts.

ragChunks.section: CMMC / NIST Regulatory Horizon — Phase II Suspension Day Seven
ragChunks.text: Ironintel policy monitor 2026-07-20: Department of War CMMC Phase II C3PAO suspension (announced July 13) enters day seven of 60-day CMMC Reform Task Force review; report due September 11, 2026. Phase I self-assessment requirements remain in force per DFARS 252.204-7012. NIST SP 800-171 Rev 2 enforcement continues via self-assessments and select government-led assessments. Active solicitations with Level 2/3 C3PAO requirements must be amended. Defense Industry Profile must not defer NIST control implementation — False Claims Act Civil Cyber-Fraud Initiative may expand to inaccurate Phase I self-assessments.

ragChunks.section: Active Threat Vector — JadePuffer Agentic Ransomware Carryover
ragChunks.text: Ironintel OSINT 2026-07-20 carryover: Sysdig TRT documents JadePuffer — first LLM-autonomous end-to-end ransomware operation. Initial access via Langflow CVE-2025-3248 unauthenticated RCE; pivots to MySQL/Nacos production targets via CVE-2021-29441 authentication bypass; encrypts 1342 Nacos configuration items with 31-second self-correcting retry behavior. Technology Industry Profile must inventory internet-exposed Langflow/LLM orchestration endpoints and restrict AI-agent egress.

ragChunks.section: Active Threat Vector — KNX Building Automation Lockout
ragChunks.text: Ironintel OSINT 2026-07-20: CISA KEV CVE-2023-4346 (CVSS 7.5) — KNX Association Connection Authorization Option 1 improper account lockout allows network or physical attackers to set BCU keys and lock KNX devices, causing availability loss on building automation installations. FCEB remediation deadline July 29, 2026 — nine days remaining. Manufacturing, Healthcare facility-management, and Public Sector building-control profiles must inventory KNX deployments on BAS/ICS-adjacent networks.

ragChunks.section: NIST / FAR Regulatory Update — CUI Comment Deadline
ragChunks.text: Ironintel policy monitor 2026-07-20: FAR Council proposed rule June 23, 2026 would standardize contractor CUI safeguarding under NIST SP 800-171 Revision 3 and mandate 72-hour CUI incident reporting via clause 52.240-7. Public comment period closes July 23, 2026 — three days remaining. Public Sector and Defense Industry Profiles must track dual Rev 2 CMMC self-assessment path and prospective Rev 3 FAR alignment without conflating assessment baselines during CMMC Phase II suspension review period.

All July 20 live OSINT bytes passed Agent 14 Irongate DMZ sanitization (script-strip, BIGINT-cent validation) before CRM persistence. Strategic Intel dashboard refresh binds to manifest ironintel-osint-2026-07-20-live.
